message:
🐛 Remove fabricated kubectl edit deployment steps from 43 install missions (#1464)

These steps contained generic "kubectl edit deployment" commands that don't
reflect real installation procedures. They were auto-generated placeholders
that reduce mission quality and credibility.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -22,10 +22,6 @@
         "title": "Verify the installation",
         "description": "Check that the Aeraki pods are running successfully in the `aeraki-system` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n aeraki-system
```"
       },
-      {
-        "title": "Configure resource limits",
-        "description": "Edit the Aeraki deployment to set resource limits for the containers. This helps ensure that the pods have the necessary resources allocated.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment aeraki -n aeraki-system
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   requests:
#     cpu: "100m"
#     memory: "128Mi"
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```"
-      },
       {
         "title": "Set up monitoring",
         "description": "Integrate monitoring by deploying Prometheus and Grafana to visualize Aeraki metrics. Follow the specific instructions in the Aeraki documentation for setting this up."

@@ -25,10 +25,6 @@
       {
         "title": "Access the Argo CD API Server",
         "description": "To access the Argo CD API server, you can port-forward the service:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/argocd-server -n argocd 8080:443
```"
-      },
-      {
-        "title": "Set resource limits for Argo CD",
-        "description": "To set resource limits for Argo CD, edit the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment argocd-server -n argocd
```
Add the following under 'spec.template.spec.containers.resources':
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi
```"
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {

@@ -22,10 +22,6 @@
         "title": "Verify the installation",
         "description": "Check that the Atlantis pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace atlantis
```"
       },
-      {
-        "title": "Configure resource limits for Atlantis",
-        "description": "Update the Atlantis deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment atlantis --namespace atlantis
```
In the editor, add the following under `spec.containers.resources`:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```"
-      },
       {
         "title": "Set up port forwarding to access Atlantis",
         "description": "To access the Atlantis UI, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/atlantis --namespace atlantis 4141:4141
```"

-      {
-        "title": "Configure Cilium with resource limits",
-        "description": "Edit the Cilium deployment to set resource limits:
```bash

-      },
       {
         "title": "Set up Cilium network policies",
         "description": "Create a simple network policy to restrict traffic:
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
  - Ingress
EOF
```"

-        "title": "Configure resource limits for CoreDNS",
-        "description": "Update the CoreDNS deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit the deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment coredns --namespace coredns
```
In the editor, under the `spec.template.spec.containers` section, add:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  requests:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "250m"
```"
-      },
       {
         "title": "Expose CoreDNS service for external access",
         "description": "To access CoreDNS externally, expose it using a LoadBalancer service:
```bash
kubectl expose deployment coredns --type=LoadBalancer --name=coredns-external --namespace coredns --port=53 --target-port=53
```"

@@ -22,10 +22,6 @@

         "description": "Check that the HAMi pods are running successfully. This command lists the pods in the 'hami' namespace.
```bash

```"

-        "title": "Configure resource limits",
-        "description": "Edit the deployment to set resource limits for the HAMi pods to ensure they have adequate resources allocated. This command opens the deployment configuration in an editor.
```bash

```"
-      },
       {
         "title": "Set up RBAC for HAMi",
         "description": "Create a Role and RoleBinding to grant the necessary permissions for HAMi to operate correctly in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: hami
  name: hami-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ["*"]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/status", "pods/exec"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "delete", "update"]
EOF

kubectl create rolebinding hami-role-binding --role=hami-role --serviceaccount=hami:hami --namespace=hami
```"

```bash

```"
       },
-      {
-        "title": "Configure resource limits",
-        "description": "To ensure KubeArmor has the necessary resources, you can edit the deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to edit:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kubearmor --namespace kubearmor
```
In the editor, add the following under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 250m
    memory: 256Mi
```"
-      },
       {
         "title": "Set up RBAC for KubeArmor",
         "description": "Create a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding to allow KubeArmor to access necessary resources:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: kubearmor-role
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["*"]
    resources: ["pods", "pods/log", "namespaces"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
---
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
metadata:
  name: kubearmor-role-binding
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: kubearmor-role
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: default
    namespace: kubearmor
EOF
```"
